title: sprayDryer operation
description: Single-stage rotary-atomiser spray dryer (PHASE 1).  Two inlets (feed liquid + drying air) and two outlets (powder + exhaust air).  The operation block carries only atomiser HARDWARE; the exhaust temperature, droplet/particle sizes, drying coefficients (h, k_c) and the constant-rate drying time are RESULTS computed from the energy balance, the Friedman rotary-disc correlation and Ranz-Marshall transfer.  Needs a transport block (viscosity + thermalConductivity + diffusivity) in the thermoPackage for the drying kinetics.

wheelDiameter.title: Atomiser disc diameter
wheelDiameter.description: Diameter of the spinning atomiser wheel.  With the feed rate it sets the liquid loading per unit wetted perimeter, hence the droplet size.

wheelSpeed.title: Atomiser disc speed
wheelSpeed.description: Rotational speed of the atomiser wheel.  Faster spinning -> smaller droplets (d32 ~ N^-0.6 in the Friedman correlation).  Industrial discs run 10000-30000 rpm.

liquidViscosity.title: Feed liquid viscosity
liquidViscosity.description: Dynamic viscosity of the feed liquid, used in the atomisation correlation.  Defaults to water (1e-3 Pa.s) -- Choupo does not yet carry liquid viscosities per component.

surfaceTension.title: Feed surface tension
surfaceTension.description: Surface tension of the feed liquid, used in the atomisation correlation.  Defaults to water (0.072 N/m).

spreadParameter.title: PSD spread (Rosin-Rammler n)
spreadParameter.description: Width of the powder particle-size distribution.  The droplet sizes follow a Rosin-Rammler Q(d)=1-exp(-(d/d')^n) around d32; larger n = narrower distribution.  Rotary atomisers typically n ~ 2-2.5.  Calibratable to measured PSD data.

chamberDiameter.title: Drying-chamber diameter
chamberDiameter.description: Diameter of the drying chamber (HARDWARE).  Sets the gas superficial velocity v_gas = Q_gas/(pi D^2/4), hence the particle fall velocity and the residence time.  The residence time is a RESULT, not a parameter.

chamberHeight.title: Drying-chamber height
chamberHeight.description: Height the particle falls through (HARDWARE).  Residence time = chamberHeight / particle fall velocity; with a sorption isotherm on the solid this closes the residual moisture.  The design question 'how tall must the chamber be to dry the powder?' is a sweep over this.

flow.title: Gas/droplet flow direction
flow.description: co-current (gas and droplets fall together, v_particle = v_gas + v_terminal) or counter-current (v_particle = v_terminal - v_gas).  Co-current is the common spray-dryer arrangement.
